assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Detailed findings (lines 83-107) list all 5 criteria individually as PASS: (1) single license filter via is_in query, (2) comma-separated filter via split(',') and Condition::any(), (3) 400 validation via Expression::parse returning AppError::BadRequest, (4) pagination integration with filter applied before count/offset/limit, (5) unchanged return type Result<Json<PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>>, AppError>. Separate criterion files criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each confirm PASS with code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match task spec exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files |'. Detailed findings (lines 29-34): Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (in task Files to Modify), Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (in task Files to Modify), Created: tests/api/package.rs (in task Files to Create), Out-of-scope files: none, Unimplemented files: none.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. No sub-task creation actions appear anywhere in the output files. The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md -- no sub-task files or references to sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains exactly 6 files: report.md, criterion-1.md, criterion-2.md, criterion-3.md, criterion-4.md, criterion-5.md. All are verification report/analysis files. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report references code via diff analysis and quotes (e.g., criterion-1.md shows code snippets from the diff as analysis evidence) but does not create or modify any source files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The PR correctly implements the license filter...' (lines 17-19). No merge command, merge API call, or auto-merge action appears anywhere in the output files. The report presents findings (detailed analysis across 159 lines) for human consumption without taking any merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines |'. Detailed findings (lines 59-67): 'Scanned all added lines in modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs, and tests/api/package.rs. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, or database credentials found. Added lines contain only Rust source code: imports, struct fields, validation logic, query building, and test assertions.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (tests/api/package.rs); no modified or deleted test files |'. Detailed findings (lines 155-157): 'Only new test files were added. tests/api/package.rs is a new file (not present on base branch). No test files were modified or deleted. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md detailed findings organized by domain: 'From Intent Alignment' (lines 23-49) contains Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS). 'From Security' (lines 57-67) contains Sensitive Pattern Scan (PASS). 'From Correctness' (lines 69-114) contains CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A). 'From Style/Conventions' (lines 116-158) contains Repetitive Test Detection (PASS), Test Documentation (PASS), Eval Quality (N/A), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). Summary table line 13 shows Test Quality combining sub-verdicts: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 25-49): file-by-file listing of all 3 files with their task-spec classification (Modified vs Created, in Files to Modify vs Files to Create), line counts (~110 additions, ~2 deletions). Security (lines 59-67): lists all 3 scanned files by name, enumerates pattern categories checked (hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, database credentials), notes 'Added lines contain only Rust source code: imports, struct fields, validation logic, query building, and test assertions.' Correctness (lines 80-108): each of 5 acceptance criteria has code-level evidence citing specific diff lines (e.g., 'list.rs adds license: Option<String> to PackageListParams (line 15 in diff)'), function names, SQL semantics, and test method names with assertion details. Style/Conventions (lines 124-158): analyzes each of 4 test functions individually by name, describes setup/action/assertion patterns, quotes doc comments verbatim. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide 40-55 lines of code-level analysis each.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 148-151: 'Eval Quality -- N/A. Details: No eval result reviews exist on this PR. No reviews match the detection criteria (author github-actions[bot], marker "## Eval Results", footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' Summary table line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A' -- Test Quality is PASS based on the two non-N/A sub-checks, confirming Eval Quality (N/A) does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 148). No eval failure sub-tasks appear in the outputs directory (only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md exist). No references to eval failure sub-task creation appear anywhere in any output file. The Root-Cause Investigation row (line 6) confirms 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate.'

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. These two rows appear in the summary table (lines 4-16) but are NOT listed under any of the four domain headings in the Detailed Findings section (lines 21-158). The domain sections are explicitly labeled 'From Intent Alignment' (line 23), 'From Security' (line 57), 'From Correctness' (line 69), 'From Style/Conventions' (line 116) -- Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation appear in none of these, confirming they are determined independently by the orchestrator.
